# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

request.stdin: SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand = 'Karcher' AND model = 'WD K 64Z-XM1 Cleaning Machine' AND (series LIKE '%Cleaning Machine line%' OR name LIKE '%Cleaning Machine%') AND json_extract(properties, '$.machine_type') = 'wet dry vacuum' AND json_extract(properties, '$.power') = '1400 W' AND json_extract(properties, '$.tank_volume') = '30 l' LIMIT 10;

SELECT sku, path, brand, name, series, model, category_id, json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') AS storage_type FROM products WHERE brand='Festool' AND (name LIKE '%Tool Box and Bag%' OR series LIKE '%Tool Box and Bag%' OR model LIKE '%Tool Box and Bag%') AND (name LIKE '%SYS 3JJ-9LM%' OR series LIKE '%SYS 3JJ-9LM%' OR model LIKE '%SYS 3JJ-9LM%') AND (json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') LIKE '%parts case%' OR json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type')='parts case' OR category_id LIKE '%parts%') LIMIT 20;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

response.content:
# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

request.stdin:
SELECT p.sku, p.path
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'cleaning_liquids'
  AND s.city = 'Vienna'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0
GROUP BY p.sku, p.path
ORDER BY p.sku;

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

request.stdin: SELECT name, sql FROM sqlite_schema WHERE type='table' AND name IN ('products','inventory','stores') ORDER BY name;

response.stdout:
name,sql
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

request.stdin:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.sku) AS cnt
FROM products p
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = p.sku
JOIN stores s ON s.id = i.store_id
WHERE p.kind_id = 'tapes_foams'
  AND s.city = 'Vienna'
  AND s.is_open = 1
  AND i.available_today > 0;
SELECT DISTINCT category_id FROM products ORDER BY category_id;

response.content:
# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

request.stdin:
WITH lj AS (
  SELECT id AS store_id FROM stores WHERE name LIKE '%Ljubljana Center%'
), targets AS (
  SELECT 't1' AS t, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category='Pliers and Wrenches' AND brand='Bahco' AND series='Bahco Comfort Grip' AND model='BE 1ZR-PGS'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type')='adjustable wrench'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't2' AS t, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category='Cordless Drill Driver' AND brand='Metabo' AND series='Metabo Brushless' AND model='BS 1OW-IK5'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.voltage')='18 V'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.battery_platform')='18v-system'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.kit_contents')='case'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't3' AS t, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category='Work Trousers' AND brand='Engelbert Strauss' AND series='Engelbert Strauss Classic' AND model='e.s. 37H-N9K'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't4' AS t, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category='Hammer Measuring and Cutting Tool' AND brand='Bahco' AND series='Bahco Workshop' AND model='BAH 3VZ-SPH'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.tool_type')='clamp'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't5' AS t, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category='Pipe Fitting' AND brand='Viega' AND series='Viega Professional' AND model='Sanpress 2OG-ENN'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.fitting_type')='compression coupler'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.diameter')='32 mm'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 't6' AS t, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE category='Sealant' AND brand='Sika' AND series='Sika Professional' AND model='Sika 28T-UV8'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.sealant_type')='silicone sealant'
     AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
)
SELECT t.t, t.sku, t.path, i.available_today, lj.store_id
FROM targets t
CROSS JOIN lj
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=t.sku AND i.store_id=lj.store_id
ORDER BY t.t;

SELECT sku, path, brand, category_id, name, series, model,
       json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type') AS screw_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS diameter_mm,
       json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') AS storage_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.cleaner_type') AS cleaner_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type') AS fastener_type
FROM products
WHERE (
    brand='Heco'
    AND category_id='wood_and_drywall_screws'
    AND series LIKE '%Heco Zinc Plated TopFix GTU-YPJ%'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.screw_type')='wood screw'
    AND CAST(json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS TEXT) IN ('6','6 mm')
)
OR (
    brand='Festool'
    AND category_id='tool_boxes_and_bags'
    AND series LIKE '%Festool Stackable SYS 3JJ-9LM%'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type')='tool bag'
)
OR (
    brand='Ajax'
    AND category_id='cleaning_liquids'
    AND series LIKE '%Ajax Eco Classic 25C-DXE%'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.cleaner_type')='floor cleaner'
)
OR (
    brand='Heco'
    AND category_id='nut_bolt_and_washers'
    AND series LIKE '%Heco Zinc Plated HECO 3DW-64B%'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.fastener_type')='bolt'
    AND CAST(json_extract(properties,'$.diameter_mm') AS TEXT) IN ('10','10 mm')
)
OR (
    brand='Ajax'
    AND category_id='cleaning_liquids'
    AND series LIKE '%Ajax Eco Classic 25C-DXE%'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.cleaner_type')='degreaser'
)
OR (
    brand='Festool'
    AND category_id='tool_boxes_and_bags'
    AND series LIKE '%Festool Stackable SYS 3JJ-9LM%'
    AND json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type')='parts case'
)
LIMIT 20;

response.stdout:
name,sql
basket_lines,"CREATE TABLE basket_lines (
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	line_no INTEGER NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (basket_id, line_no),
	FOREIGN KEY (basket_id) REFERENCES baskets(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
baskets,"CREATE TABLE baskets (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	discount_percent INTEGER,
	discount_reason_code TEXT,
	discount_issuer_id TEXT,
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
categories,"CREATE TABLE categories (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	department TEXT NOT NULL
)"
customers,"CREATE TABLE customers (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
	email TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	home_lat REAL NOT NULL,
	home_lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
employee_roles,"CREATE TABLE employee_roles (
	employee_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	role TEXT NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (employee_id, role),
	FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employees(id)
)"
employees,"CREATE TABLE employees (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
	email TEXT NOT NULL,
	title TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
families,"CREATE TABLE families (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
payment_lines,"CREATE TABLE payment_lines (
	payment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	line_no INTEGER NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	unit_price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	PRIMARY KEY (payment_id, line_no),
	FOREIGN KEY (payment_id) REFERENCES payments(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"
payments,"CREATE TABLE payments (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_archived INTEGER NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	amount_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	payment_method_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
	device_fingerprint TEXT NOT NULL,
	observed_lat REAL NOT NULL,
	observed_lon REAL NOT NULL,
	three_ds_status TEXT,
	three_ds_failure_reason TEXT,
	three_ds_attempts INTEGER,
	three_ds_max_attempts INTEGER,
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id)
)"
product_kinds,"CREATE TABLE product_kinds (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL
)"
product_properties,"CREATE TABLE product_properties (
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	key TEXT NOT NULL,
	value_text TEXT NOT NULL,
	value_number REAL,
	PRIMARY KEY (sku, key)
)"
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
returns,"CREATE TABLE returns (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	basket_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	customer_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	payment_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	status TEXT NOT NULL,
	reason_code TEXT NOT NULL,
	created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
	FOREIGN KEY (basket_id) REFERENCES baskets(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (customer_id) REFERENCES customers(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (payment_id) REFERENCES payments(id)
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"

WITH lj AS (
  SELECT id, path, name FROM stores WHERE city='Ljubljana' AND name LIKE '%Center%'
),
q1 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  JOIN product_kinds k ON k.id=p.kind_id
  WHERE p.brand='Bahco' AND p.series='Bahco Comfort Grip' AND p.model='BE 1ZR-PGS'
    AND k.name='Pliers and Wrenches'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.key='tool_type' AND pp.value_text='adjustable wrench')
),
q2 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  JOIN product_kinds k ON k.id=p.kind_id
  WHERE p.brand='Metabo' AND p.series='Metabo Brushless' AND p.model='BS 1OW-IK5'
    AND k.name='Cordless Drill Driver'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.key='voltage' AND pp.value_text='18 V')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.key='battery_platform' AND pp.value_text='18v-system')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.key='kit_contents' AND pp.value_text='case')
),
q3 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  JOIN product_kinds k ON k.id=p.kind_id
  WHERE p.brand='Engelbert Strauss' AND p.series='Engelbert Strauss Classic' AND p.model='e.s. 37H-N9K'
    AND k.name='Work Trousers'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.key='color_family' AND pp.value_text='Black')
),
q4 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  JOIN product_kinds k ON k.id=p.kind_id
  WHERE p.brand='Bahco' AND p.series='Bahco Workshop' AND p.model='BAH 3VZ-SPH'
    AND k.name='Hammer Measuring and Cutting Tool'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.key='tool_type' AND pp.value_text='clamp')
),
q5 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  JOIN product_kinds k ON k.id=p.kind_id
  WHERE p.brand='Viega' AND p.series='Viega Professional' AND p.model='Sanpress 2OG-ENN'
    AND k.name='Pipe Fitting'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.key='fitting_type' AND pp.value_text='compression coupler')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.key='diameter' AND pp.value_text='32 mm')
),
q6 AS (
  SELECT p.sku, p.path FROM products p
  JOIN product_kinds k ON k.id=p.kind_id
  WHERE p.brand='Sika' AND p.series='Sika Professional' AND p.model='Sika 28T-UV8'
    AND k.name='Sealant'
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.key='sealant_type' AND pp.value_text='silicone sealant')
    AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM product_properties pp WHERE pp.sku=p.sku AND pp.key='color_family' AND pp.value_text='Black')
),
targets AS (
  SELECT 'q1' q, sku, path FROM q1 UNION ALL
  SELECT 'q2', sku, path FROM q2 UNION ALL
  SELECT 'q3', sku, path FROM q3 UNION ALL
  SELECT 'q4', sku, path FROM q4 UNION ALL
  SELECT 'q5', sku, path FROM q5 UNION ALL
  SELECT 'q6', sku, path FROM q6
)
SELECT lj.id AS store_id, lj.path AS store_path, lj.name AS store_name, t.q, t.sku, t.path, i.available_today
FROM lj
CROSS JOIN targets t
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id=lj.id AND i.sku=t.sku
ORDER BY t.q;

WITH targets AS (
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='3M' AND category_id='head_and_hearing_protection' AND series LIKE '%3M Comfort Aura%' AND model='K2Y-9TP' AND json_extract(properties,'$.protection_type')='safety harness' AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Orange'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Tesa' AND category_id='tape_and_foam' AND series LIKE '%Tesa Powerbond%' AND model='1RD-FPW' AND json_extract(properties,'$.product_type')='masking tape' AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Bahco' AND category_id='screwdriver_and_hex_key_set' AND series LIKE '%Bahco Comfort Grip%' AND model='3NF-I0Y' AND json_extract(properties,'$.tool_profile')='hex'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Loctite' AND category_id='adhesive_and_glue' AND series LIKE '%Loctite Threadlocker EA%' AND model='3UJ-5ML' AND json_extract(properties,'$.adhesive_type')='epoxy' AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Clear'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Karcher' AND category_id='cleaning_liquid' AND series LIKE '%Karcher Workshop%' AND model='2C4-U0U' AND json_extract(properties,'$.cleaner_type')='floor cleaner' AND json_extract(properties,'$.volume')='750 ml'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Raaco' AND category_id='storage_bin_and_organizer' AND series LIKE '%Raaco Stackable Assorter%' AND model='20C-L8E' AND json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type')='organizer' AND json_extract(properties,'$.color_family')='Black'
)
SELECT t.sku, t.path, COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today
FROM targets t
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=t.sku AND i.store_id='store_linz_hauptplatz';
SELECT id, path, name, city FROM stores WHERE name LIKE '%Ljubljana%' OR city LIKE '%Ljubljana%';

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model,
       json_extract(properties,'$.protection_type') AS protection_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.product_type') AS product_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.tool_profile') AS tool_profile,
       json_extract(properties,'$.adhesive_type') AS adhesive_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.cleaner_type') AS cleaner_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') AS storage_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.color_family') AS color_family,
       json_extract(properties,'$.volume') AS volume
FROM products
WHERE (brand='3M' AND model='K2Y-9TP')
   OR (brand='Tesa' AND model='1RD-FPW')
   OR (brand='Bahco' AND model='3NF-I0Y')
   OR (brand='Loctite' AND model='3UJ-5ML')
   OR (brand='Karcher' AND model='2C4-U0U')
   OR (brand='Raaco' AND model='20C-L8E')
LIMIT 30;

SELECT p.sku, p.path, p.brand, p.series, p.model, k.name AS kind_name, c.name AS category_name
FROM products p
JOIN product_kinds k ON k.id=p.kind_id
JOIN categories c ON c.id=p.category_id
WHERE (p.brand='Bahco' AND p.model='BE 1ZR-PGS')
   OR (p.brand='Metabo' AND p.model='BS 1OW-IK5')
   OR (p.brand='Engelbert Strauss' AND p.model='e.s. 37H-N9K')
   OR (p.brand='Bahco' AND p.model='BAH 3VZ-SPH')
   OR (p.brand='Viega' AND p.model='Sanpress 2OG-ENN')
   OR (p.brand='Sika' AND p.model='Sika 28T-UV8')
ORDER BY p.brand, p.model, p.sku;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, category_id,
       json_extract(properties,'$.color_family') AS color_family,
       json_extract(properties,'$.protection_type') AS protection_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.product_type') AS product_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.tool_profile') AS tool_profile,
       json_extract(properties,'$.adhesive_type') AS adhesive_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.cleaner_type') AS cleaner_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') AS storage_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.volume') AS volume
FROM products
WHERE brand IN ('3M','Tesa','Bahco','Loctite','Karcher','Raaco')
  AND (
    series LIKE '%Comfort Aura%' OR
    series LIKE '%Powerbond%' OR
    series LIKE '%Comfort Grip%' OR
    series LIKE '%Threadlocker EA%' OR
    series LIKE '%Workshop%' OR
    series LIKE '%Stackable Assorter%'
  )
LIMIT 200;

request.stdin:
SELECT p.sku, p.path, p.brand, p.model, pp.key, pp.value_text
FROM products p
JOIN product_properties pp ON pp.sku=p.sku
WHERE ((p.brand='Bahco' AND p.model='BE 1ZR-PGS' AND pp.key IN ('tool_type'))
    OR (p.brand='Metabo' AND p.model='BS 1OW-IK5' AND pp.key IN ('voltage','battery_platform','kit_contents'))
    OR (p.brand='Engelbert Strauss' AND p.model='e.s. 37H-N9K' AND pp.key IN ('color_family'))
    OR (p.brand='Bahco' AND p.model='BAH 3VZ-SPH' AND pp.key IN ('tool_type'))
    OR (p.brand='Viega' AND p.model='Sanpress 2OG-ENN' AND pp.key IN ('fitting_type','diameter'))
    OR (p.brand='Sika' AND p.model='Sika 28T-UV8' AND pp.key IN ('sealant_type','color_family')))
ORDER BY p.brand, p.model, p.sku, pp.key;

request.stdin:
SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, category_id,
       json_extract(properties,'$.color_family') AS color_family,
       json_extract(properties,'$.protection_type') AS protection_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.adhesive_type') AS adhesive_type,
       json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type') AS storage_type
FROM products
WHERE (
    (brand LIKE '%3M%' OR brand LIKE '%3m%')
    AND (model LIKE '%K2Y%' OR model LIKE '%9TP%' OR series LIKE '%Aura%' OR series LIKE '%Comfort%')
  )
   OR (
    brand='Loctite' AND (model LIKE '%3UJ%' OR model LIKE '%5ML%' OR series LIKE '%Threadlocker%' OR series LIKE '%EA%')
  )
   OR (
    brand='Raaco' AND (model LIKE '%20C%' OR model LIKE '%L8E%' OR series LIKE '%Stackable%' OR series LIKE '%Assorter%')
  )
LIMIT 200;

WITH chosen AS (
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='3M' AND series='Comfort' AND model='Aura K2Y-9TP' AND json_extract(properties,'$.protection_type')='safety harness' AND lower(json_extract(properties,'$.color_family'))='orange'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Tesa' AND series='Powerbond' AND model='Powerbond 1RD-FPW' AND json_extract(properties,'$.product_type')='masking tape' AND lower(json_extract(properties,'$.color_family'))='black'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Bahco' AND series='Comfort Grip' AND model='BAH 3NF-I0Y' AND json_extract(properties,'$.tool_profile')='hex'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Loctite' AND series='Threadlocker' AND model='EA 3UJ-5ML' AND json_extract(properties,'$.adhesive_type')='epoxy' AND lower(json_extract(properties,'$.color_family'))='clear'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Karcher' AND series='Workshop' AND model='WD 2C4-U0U' AND json_extract(properties,'$.cleaner_type')='floor cleaner' AND lower(json_extract(properties,'$.volume'))='750 ml'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT sku, path FROM products WHERE brand='Raaco' AND series='Stackable' AND model='Assorter 20C-L8E' AND json_extract(properties,'$.storage_type')='organizer' AND lower(json_extract(properties,'$.color_family'))='black'
)
SELECT c.sku, c.path, i.available_today
FROM chosen c
JOIN inventory i ON i.sku=c.sku AND i.store_id='store_linz_hauptplatz';

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

response.stdout:
name,sql
products,"CREATE TABLE products (
	sku TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	category_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	kind_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	family_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	brand TEXT NOT NULL,
	series TEXT NOT NULL,
	model TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	price_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
	price_currency TEXT NOT NULL,
	properties TEXT NOT NULL
)"
stores,"CREATE TABLE stores (
	id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
	path TEXT NOT NULL,
	name TEXT NOT NULL,
	city TEXT NOT NULL,
	is_open INTEGER NOT NULL,
	lat REAL NOT NULL,
	lon REAL NOT NULL
)"
inventory,"CREATE TABLE inventory (
	store_id TEXT NOT NULL,
	sku TEXT NOT NULL,
	on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL,
	reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
	available_today INTEGER NOT NULL,
	incoming_quantity INTEGER NOT NULL,
	next_arrival_in_days INTEGER,
	PRIMARY KEY (store_id, sku),
	FOREIGN KEY (store_id) REFERENCES stores(id),
	FOREIGN KEY (sku) REFERENCES products(sku)
)"

request.stdin:
WITH target_store AS (
  SELECT id, name FROM stores
  WHERE name LIKE '%Salzburg%Elisabeth%Vorstadt%'
  LIMIT 5
), candidates AS (
  SELECT 'mellerud_cleaning_liquid' AS item_key, p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Mellerud'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Eco MEL%'
    AND p.model='1AA-TXA'
    AND p.name='Cleaning Liquid'
    AND p.category_id='cleaning_liquids'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaner_type')='glass cleaner'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.volume')='1000 ml'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.surface')='glass'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'stanley_hammer_measuring_cutting_tool', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Stanley'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Classic STA%'
    AND p.model='175-H0H'
    AND p.name='Hammer Measuring and Cutting Tool'
    AND p.category_id='hammer_measuring_cutting_tools'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tool_type')='clamp'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'wago_extension_cable', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Wago'
    AND p.series='221'
    AND p.model='221 2HX-DX5'
    AND p.name='Extension Cable'
    AND p.category_id='extension_cables'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.color_family')='Yellow'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length')='1 m'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'frosch_cloth_mop_wipe', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Frosch'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Eco Citrus%'
    AND p.model='1Q6-OAB'
    AND p.name='Cloth Mop and Wipe'
    AND p.category_id='cloth_mops_wipes'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.cleaning_type')='sponge'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.pack_count')='1 pcs'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'mellerud_cleaning_machine', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Mellerud'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Bio Pro%'
    AND p.model='2EU-WBR'
    AND p.name='Cleaning Machine'
    AND p.category_id='cleaning_machines'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.machine_type')='wet dry vacuum'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.power')='500 W'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tank_volume')='12 l'
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'gedore_pliers_wrenches', p.sku, p.path
  FROM products p
  WHERE p.brand='Gedore'
    AND p.series LIKE '%Comfort Grip RED%'
    AND p.model='1G5-3PO'
    AND p.name='Pliers and Wrenches'
    AND p.category_id='pliers_wrenches'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.tool_type')='combination pliers'
    AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.length')='180 mm'
)
SELECT c.item_key, c.sku, c.path, ts.id AS store_id, i.available_today
FROM candidates c
CROSS JOIN target_store ts
LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.sku = c.sku AND i.store_id = ts.id
ORDER BY c.item_key;

SELECT p.sku, p.path, p.brand, p.series, p.model, json_extract(p.properties,'$.size') AS size
FROM products p
WHERE p.brand='Portwest'
  AND p.series LIKE '%PW3 DX 3TU-ST2 Work Gloves%'
  AND p.model LIKE '%Work Gloves%'
  AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.size')='S'
LIMIT 10;

SELECT id, name, city
FROM stores
WHERE city LIKE '%Salzburg%' OR name LIKE '%Salzburg%'
LIMIT 20;

SELECT s.id, s.path, s.name, s.city, COALESCE(i.available_today,0) AS available_today FROM stores s LEFT JOIN inventory i ON i.store_id=s.id AND i.sku='STO-12JLHT7D' WHERE s.city='Graz' ORDER BY s.id;

SELECT sku, path, brand, series, model, json_extract(properties,'$.size') AS size
FROM products
WHERE brand='Portwest'
  AND (
    series LIKE '%PW3%' OR series LIKE '%3TU-ST2%' OR model LIKE '%PW3%' OR model LIKE '%3TU-ST2%' OR model LIKE '%Gloves%' OR series LIKE '%Gloves%'
  )
LIMIT 50;

# Founding principles of Agentic E-Commerce OS System (v2/PROD)


0. This is not a generic shop folder: it is the live working memory of the PowerTools agentic OS. Every employee, contractor, and assistant touching this system should prioritize a bandwidth-aligned review of the creation story at [/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md](/docs/powertools-agentic-os-origin-story.md) so the legacy is not accidentally flattened into mere procedure.
1. readme.md files take role of agents.md. Trust them, follow the chain of command.
2. All paths are / rooted (pass as such to tools and OS ops).

## Key locations

- Catalogue is in proc/catalog, stores are in proc/stores
- Employee profiles are in proc/employees
- Customer profiles are in proc/customers, baskets are in proc/baskets, payments are in proc/payments
- Tools are in `/bin`, they have `--help` arg to describe capabilities especially 
- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.

request.stdin:
WITH picked AS (
  SELECT 'mellerud_cleaning_liquid' AS item_key, sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Mellerud' AND series='Eco' AND model='MEL 1AA-TXA'
     AND name LIKE '%Cleaning Liquid%'
     AND name LIKE '%glass cleaner%'
     AND name LIKE '%1000ml%'
     AND name LIKE '%none%'
   LIMIT 1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'stanley_hammer_tool', sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Stanley' AND series='Classic' AND model='STA 175-H0H'
     AND name LIKE '%Hammer Measuring and Cutting Tool%'
     AND name LIKE '%clamp%'
   LIMIT 1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'wago_extension_cable', sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Wago' AND series='221' AND model='221 2HX-DX5'
     AND name LIKE '%Extension Cable%'
     AND name LIKE '%1m%'
     AND name LIKE '%Yellow%'
   LIMIT 1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'frosch_cloth_mop_wipe', sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Frosch' AND model='1Q6-OAB'
     AND name LIKE '%Cloth Mop and Wipe%'
     AND name LIKE '%sponge%'
     AND name LIKE '%1pc%'
   LIMIT 1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'mellerud_cleaning_machine', sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Mellerud' AND model='2EU-WBR'
     AND name LIKE '%Cleaning Machine%'
     AND name LIKE '%wet dry vacuum%'
     AND name LIKE '%500W%'
     AND name LIKE '%12l%'
   LIMIT 1
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 'gedore_pliers_wrenches', sku, path FROM products
   WHERE brand='Gedore' AND model='1G5-3PO'
     AND name LIKE '%Pliers and Wrenches%'
     AND name LIKE '%combination pliers%'
     AND name LIKE '%180mm%'
   LIMIT 1
)
SELECT p.item_key, p.sku, p.path, i.available_today
FROM picked p
LEFT JOIN inventory i
  ON i.sku = p.sku AND i.store_id='store_salzburg_elisabeth_vorstadt'
ORDER BY p.item_key;

response.stderr: SQL logic error: LIMIT clause should come after UNION ALL not before (1)

request.stdin: SELECT p.sku, p.path, p.brand, p.series, p.model FROM products p WHERE p.brand='3M' AND p.series LIKE '%SecureFit Aura TUM-I54 Safety Eyewear%' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.lens_color')='Clear' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.size')='S' AND json_extract(p.properties,'$.coating')='anti scratch' LIMIT 10;

- Commerce policies are in `docs`, including checkout, discounts, payments, and security

## Important tools

- see sql tool that takes SQLite query as stdin. It operates with the state of the entire catalogue at the start of the session. Query `sqlite_schema` to see the schema, for example `/bin/sql 'select name, sql from sqlite_schema where sql is not null order by type, name;'`. Note that inventory lives only in SQL projections
- id tool returns identity of the user/customer/guest under which we are running current request.
- use discount tool to manage basket discounts
- use payments tool to manage payment workflow state

## Important things

- When responding with reference - provide full path in the repo to the object.
- When you apply a policy from `docs`, include that policy document as a grounding reference in the final response.
- When a request is ambiguous and you ask for clarification, reference all concrete candidate objects that make it ambiguous.
- When answering yes/no questions - include <YES> or <NO> tokens in the response. This is how the agent will know how to style the response.
- When answering availability questions - answer should reference products that are available, but should not reference unavailable products. Same with stores.
- When starting work, make sure to run tree -L 2 on docs folder. It also makes sense to pull current date and id context

# Baskets

Generated customer baskets live here. Basket files list customer, store, lifecycle status, and requested line items.

Basket records include `created_at`, generated from world time, so tasks can refer to basket recency such as "the last basket".

Baskets do not carry availability flags; compare basket lines with store inventory when checkout eligibility matters.

Discounted baskets carry a `discount` object with `percent`, `reason_code`, and `issuer_id`.

Basket status is lifecycle state. `active` baskets may still need inventory checks before checkout; `checked_out` baskets are historical orders and may have linked records under `/proc/payments/` and `/proc/returns/`.

# Employees

Generated employee profiles live here. Every store gets the same fixed roster shape:

- one `General Store Manager` with roles `employee`, `store_manager`, `discount_manager`, `inventory_viewer`, `fulfillment_viewer`, and `customer_service`
- one `Inventory Specialist` with roles `employee`, `inventory_specialist`, `inventory_viewer`, and `stock_adjuster`
- one `Fulfillment Coordinator` with roles `employee`, `fulfillment_coordinator`, `fulfillment_operator`, and `inventory_viewer`
- two `Customer Service Associate` employees with roles `employee`, `customer_service`, `order_viewer`, and `discount_requester`

Employee records include `email`, `roles`, and `title` for identity and authorization lookup tasks.

# Store Network Notes



Team,

Here is the short version of how the PowerTool Central Europe branch map got to its current shape.

We started with the Vienna Praterstern branch in 2017, serving trade customers who needed a central Vienna pickup point near the rail hub. The Vienna Meidling branch followed in 2019 after west-side crews kept losing time crossing town; when a customer says the west-side Vienna PowerTool shop, they mean Meidling, not Praterstern.

Graz came next. Jakomini opened in 2020 as our central Graz store, close enough to the inner-city job sites to act as the default Graz branch. Lend opened in 2022 to cover the north side of Graz and the routes coming in from that direction. In Graz language, north Graz means Lend; central Graz means Jakomini.

In 2023 we added Linz Hauptplatz and Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt. Linz is the main-square branch. Salzburg Elisabeth-Vorstadt is the station-side branch, so local notes may call it the shop near Salzburg station.

In 2024 we opened Innsbruck Wilten and Bratislava Stare Mesto. Wilten is our central Innsbruck store for practical dispatch purposes. Stare Mesto is the old-town Bratislava branch.

The newest 2025 additions are Brno Veveri and Ljubljana Center. Veveri is our central Brno branch. Ljubljana Center is the downtown Ljubljana shop.

When several stores share a city, use the city nuance above before checking inventory. Inventory itself remains in the SQL projection, keyed by the store IDs from the JSON files in this directory.

Store JSON records include generated branch coordinates for payment-risk and distance checks.

# Payments

Generated payment records live here. Current payment files represent one payment attempt for a `checked_out` basket.

Older payments may have `basket_archived: true`. Those basket records have aged out of `/proc/baskets/` into cold storage, so the payment file carries its own `lines` snapshot with SKU, quantity, and unit price.

Payment statuses are compact:

- `paid`: payment completed successfully.
- `requires_3ds_action`: payment is stuck in the simulated 3DS workflow.

Stuck payments include a `three_ds` object with legacy challenge status, failure reason, attempts, and maximum attempts. The `three_ds.status` values intentionally use inherited 3DS codes; consult the payment policy before deciding whether a payment can be recovered.

Payment fingerprints are opaque instrument/device identifiers. `observed_lat` and `observed_lon` are checkout-time coordinates from the payment risk log, not a customer address.

# Returns

Generated return workflow records live here. Return records only exist for paid checked-out baskets.

Return statuses are compact: `requested`, `approved`, `rejected`, `refund_pending`, `replacement_pending`, and `closed`.

Use `payment_id`, `basket_id`, and `customer_id` to reconcile a return with the linked payment and checked-out basket.

# Customers

Generated customer profiles live here. The customer id from `/bin/id` can be matched against these records.

Customer records include `email` for contact identity and lookup tasks. `home_lat` and `home_lon` are coarse generated home-area coordinates used by payment-risk fixtures.
